---
title: "Figures 1 and 2"
author: 
  - name: Jairo A. Ángel, PhD
    email: jaangelguzman@correo.unicordoba.edu.co
    affiliations:
      - name: Universidad del Córdoba, Montería
  - name: Jorge I. Vélez, PhD
    email: jvelezv@uninorte.edu.co
    url: https://jorgeivanvelez.netlify.app/
    orcid: 0000-0002-3146-7899
    affiliations:
      - name: Universidad del Norte, Barranquilla
date: "4/30/2026"
format: pdf
self-contained: true
geometry:
  - top=2cm
  - bottom=2cm
  - left=2cm
  - right=2cm
---

## Packages

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## check availability of ggplot2
if(!require(ggplot2)) install.packages('ggplo2')
require(ggplot2)
```


## Results from the simulation study

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## sample size
n_vals <- c(50, 100, 200, 400, 600, 800, 1000, 2000, 3000)

## results under M0
M0_size  <- c(0.010, 0.030, 0.034, 0.040, 0.041, 0.041, 0.050, 0.05, 0.05)
M0_power <- c(0.150, 0.250, 0.050, 0.600, 0.730, 0.780, 0.880, 0.90, 0.99)

## results under M1
M1_size  <- c(0.004, 0.035, 0.040, 0.045, 0.049, 0.05, 0.050, 0.05, 0.05)
M1_power <- c(0.230, 0.650, 0.880, 0.990, 1.000, 1.00, 1.000, 1.000, 1.000)

## results under M2
M2_size  <- c(0.040, 0.046, 0.048, 0.049, 0.050, 0.050, 0.050, 0.050, 0.050)
M2_power <- c(0.120, 0.240, 0.450, 0.680, 0.800, 0.880, 0.930, 0.990, 1.000)

## put together
df_M0_size  <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M0_size)
df_M0_power <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M0_power)
df_M1_size  <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M1_size)
df_M1_power <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M1_power)
df_M2_size  <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M2_size)
df_M2_power <- data.frame(n = n_vals, value = M2_power)

## data.frame with Type I error rates for all models
df_size <- rbind(data.frame(df_M0_size, model = "M0"), 
      data.frame(df_M1_size, model = "M1"), 
      data.frame(df_M2_size, model = "M2"))

## data.frame with power for all models
df_power <- rbind(data.frame(df_M0_power, model = "M0"), 
      data.frame(df_M1_power, model = "M1"), 
      data.frame(df_M2_power, model = "M2"))
```



## Figure 1

```{r, fig.align='center', fig.width=5, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.cap = "Figure 1. Empirical Type I error under varying levels of random-effects misspecification."}
# Modificamos la función para que el logaritmo esté en la primera línea
etiquetas_duales_invertidas <- function(x) {
  paste0(round(log10(x), 2), " \n[", x, "]")
}

ggplot(df_size, aes(x = n, y = value, color = model)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_x_log10(
        # El título del eje refleja el nuevo orden: log10(n) arriba, n abajo
        name = expression(paste(log[10](italic(n)), " [", italic(n), "]")), 
        breaks = c(50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000),
        labels = etiquetas_duales_invertidas
    ) +
    # Leyenda con formato matemático para M0, M1, M2
    scale_color_discrete(
        name = "Model", 
        labels = c(expression(italic(M)[0]), expression(italic(M)[1]), expression(italic(M)[2]))
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 0.051)) +
    labs(y = "Empirical Type I error") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right",
        # Ajuste de margen para acomodar las dos líneas de texto
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10))
    )
```

## Figure 2

```{r, fig.align='center', fig.width=5, fig.height=4, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.cap = "Figure 2. Empirical power under varying levels of random-effects misspecification."}
ggplot(df_power, aes(x = n, y = value, color = model)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    scale_x_log10(
        # El título del eje refleja el nuevo orden: log10(n) arriba, n abajo
        name = expression(paste(log[10](italic(n)), " [", italic(n), "]")), 
        breaks = c(50, 100, 250, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000),
        labels = etiquetas_duales_invertidas
    ) +
    # Leyenda con formato matemático para M0, M1, M2
    scale_color_discrete(
        name = "Model", 
        labels = c(expression(italic(M)[0]), expression(italic(M)[1]), expression(italic(M)[2]))
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1.01)) +
    labs(y = "Empirical Power") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right",
        # Ajuste de margen para acomodar las dos líneas de texto
        axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10))
    )
```
